title: Ghost Travel Card: Geo-Hopping Without Transportation Anchors
narrative: A compromised card shows rapid geographic movement across countries (Brazilâ†’UAEâ†’Singapore within 72 hours) without corresponding airline, hotel, or car rental transactions that would support legitimate travel. Pattern emerges with card1_txn_count_24h spiking from baseline 0.2 to 8+ transactions, all ProductCD='C' (card-not-present). Transactions cluster at gas stations (MCC 5541), convenience stores (MCC 5499), and digital goods retailers in each location with amounts $47-$189, always avoiding travel MCCs (3000-3999, 4000-4799, 7000-7999). Dist1 values show 8000+ mile jumps between successive transactions occurring 6-14 hours apart. Email domain remains static (P_emaildomain='gmail.com') while addr1 cycles through three distinct countries. No AVS/CVV mismatch initially, suggesting complete card data compromise. Fraudster tests card domestically first, then executes international purchases once validation succeeds, avoiding merchant categories that would create contradictory travel documentation.
indicators: dist1 >8000 miles between transactions occurring <18 hours apart, minimum 2 continental jumps in 72-hour window
Zero transactions in travel MCCs (3000-3999 airlines, 4000-4799 hotels, 7011-7033 lodging) during geographic movement period
card1_txn_count_72h increases 15x+ from 30-day baseline, all ProductCD='C' card-not-present
addr1 country code changes 3+ times while P_emaildomain and R_emaildomain remain constant
Transaction amounts $40-$200 at non-travel MCCs (5541 gas, 5499 convenience, 5732 electronics) in each geography
txn_hour distribution shows purchases during local business hours (08:00-22:00) in each timezone despite impossible travel time
First international transaction occurs 4-12 hours after successful domestic test transaction at same MCC category
summary: Card shows purchases across multiple countries within 72 hours (Brazil→UAE→Singapore) with no airline, hotel, or rental car transactions to explain the travel. Transactions cluster at gas stations, convenience stores, and electronics retailers in amounts of $40-$200, always during local business hours in each timezone.
reasoning: Fraudsters using stolen card details execute purchases in geographically distant locations without leaving evidence of legitimate travel expenses. The impossibility of physical travel between locations, combined with avoidance of travel-related merchants, reveals the card is being used by multiple criminals or through international networks rather than by the actual cardholder.
indicator_explanations: Card teleports across continents within hours with no physical travel time
No airline, hotel, or lodging charges despite massive geographic movements
Card activity explodes to fifteen times normal with all online transactions
Shipping country jumps repeatedly while email addresses stay suspiciously unchanged
Small everyday purchases at gas stations and stores in each country
Purchases happen during waking hours locally in each timezone simultaneously
International fraud kicks off hours after domestic test proves card works
